answer:
**All three parts of Allama Iqbal's *Bang-e-Dara* (The Call of the Marching Bell, 1924) contain numerous poems—primarily ghazals and nazms—where Iqbal inserts his takhallus (pen name) "Iqbal" in the maqta (signature couplet) or equivalent closing lines, a standard convention in Urdu poetry to sign the work.**[^1][^2][^3]

This self-reference appears frequently across the collection, often in the form of "ae Iqbal" (O Iqbal) or direct address, as in:

- Part 1, Chapter 2 ("Anokhi waza hai..."): "O Iqbal, why should my verse seem in vain? / They are my broken heart's own cries of pain."[^4]
- Part 1, Chapter 4 ("Jinhein main dhoondta tha..."): "...for I, myself, O Iqbal, am counted among my own critics."[^5]
- Part 3, Chapter 10 is even titled "Har nafas Iqbal tera aah mein mastoor hai" ("Every breath, O Iqbal, is inscribed in your sigh"), indicating a maqta-style signature.[^6]

No other works in the corpus (e.g., *Diwan-e-Ghalib* or *Diwan-e-Zauq*) use "Iqbal" in this way, as confirmed by searches across Urdu poetry.[^7]

[^1]: *Bang-E-Dara Part 1*, Allama Iqbal, [Chapter 1](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/01-ajab-waaiz-ki-deen-daari-hai-ya-rabb/transliteration/).
[^2]: *Bang-E-Dara Part 2*, Allama Iqbal, [Chapter 1](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-2-9bc58c/01-chamak-teri-ayaan-bijli-mein-aatish-mein-sharare-m/transliteration/).
[^3]: *Bang-E-Dara Part 3*, Allama Iqbal, [Chapter 10](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/10-har-nafas-iqbal-tera-aah-mein-mastoor-hai/translation/).
[^4]: *Bang-E-Dara Part 1*, Chapter 2, [p-0118fb](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/02-anokhi-waza-hai-saare-zamane-se-nirale-hain/translation/#p-0118fb).
[^5]: *Bang-E-Dara Part 1*, Chapter 4, [p-808707](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/04-jinhein-main-dhoondta-tha-aasmanon-mein-zameenon-m/translation/#p-808707).
[^6]: *Bang-E-Dara Part 3*, Chapter 10, [full chapter](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-3-cc61e8/10-har-nafas-iqbal-tera-aah-mein-mastoor-hai/transliteration/).
[^7]: Corpus-wide search for "Iqbal" in English translations/transliterations returned 50+ hits, all confined to Iqbal's *Bang-e-Dara* parts; zero in other Urdu works like Ghalib or Zauq.
